aliases: Carrot cake with cream cheese frosting

english: Carrot cake with cream cheese frosting

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code specific to carrot cake exists; A00KP (Cakes and pastries) used as the closest defensible parent category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for carrot cake dessert; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical slice of carrot cake (approximately 90 g) as served at a café or bakery, including cream cheese frosting.

nutrient_source.title: Carrot cake, dry mix, prepared — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18101) cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo carrot cake entry (F02010004)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Health Promotion Board Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary macro values (energy 241 kcal, protein 2 g, fat 13 g, carbohydrate 30 g, sodium 175 mg, dietary fibre 1 g, calcium 28 mg, iron 0.7 mg) are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo record F02010004 (confidence 0.95). Saturated fat estimated at 4.5 g/100 g and sugar estimated at 22 g/100 g are derived from USDA FoodData Central carrot cake with cream cheese frosting (FDC ID 174929) as the closest approved secondary source. Cholesterol, potassium, and micronutrient values are populated from USDA FDC 174929 where available; vitamin_d_mcg and selenium_mcg set to -1 where no approved-source data is available.

gi_source.title: Carrot cake, GI value 39 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI value of 39 for carrot cake is sourced from the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008), published in Diabetes Care and indexed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (39 × 27 g carbohydrate in 90 g slice) / 100 = 10.5.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (22 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (1 g/100 g) promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 175 mg/100 g is moderate but cumulative intake risk is elevated.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit carrot cake to an occasional small portion (≤50 g), avoid cream cheese frosting, and pair with a high-fibre food to blunt the insulin-sodium retention response.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 22 g/100 g exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; carbohydrate at 30 g/100 g with only 1 g/100 g dietary fibre represents a high refined carbohydrate load despite a low GI of 39.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid or strictly limit carrot cake; if consumed, restrict to a single small slice (≤50 g) infrequently, monitor post-prandial glucose, and request a reduced-sugar, wholemeal-flour version without cream cheese frosting.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat at approximately 4.5 g/100 g from butter and cream cheese frosting, combined with 22 g/100 g sugar and 38 mg/100 g cholesterol, contributes to atherogenic lipid burden and endothelial inflammation risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit carrot cake to occasional small portions, preferring versions made with unsaturated oils, reduced sugar, and no cream cheese frosting to minimise saturated fat and added sugar intake.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar (22 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity; saturated fat (~4.5 g/100 g) from cream cheese frosting approaches the RA threshold for dietary fat-driven inflammation.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise carrot cake consumption; if eaten, choose a walnut-topped version without frosting to reduce sugar and saturated fat while gaining modest omega-3 and polyphenol benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Carrot cake (dessert) is a moist, spiced Western-style baked cake made with grated carrots, wheat flour, eggs, vegetable oil, sugar, and warm spices such as cinnamon and nutmeg, typically finished with a generous layer of cream cheese frosting. The cake has a tender, dense crumb with a subtly sweet, earthy flavour from the carrots and a tangy richness from the cream cheese icing. Walnuts or pecans are commonly folded into the batter, adding texture and a mild nuttiness. Per 100 g, carrot cake delivers approximately 241 kcal, 13 g fat (of which ~4.5 g is saturated), 30 g carbohydrate, 22 g sugar, 2 g protein, and 1 g dietary fibre, with 175 mg sodium. Despite a low glycemic index of 39, the high sugar content (22 g/100 g) and low fibre make it clinically unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes or hypertension without strict portion control. The cream cheese frosting significantly elevates saturated fat and sugar density. Carrot cake is widely served as a dessert or afternoon snack in cafés, bakeries, and restaurants across Singapore and internationally. It is vegetarian and typically halal, but contains gluten, dairy, eggs, and tree nuts, making it unsuitable for those with these allergies. Nutritional data sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo (F02010004) and USDA FoodData Central (FDC 174929).
common_user_queries: How many calories in a slice of carrot cake?
Is carrot cake suitable for hypertension?
What is the GI of carrot cake?
Does carrot cake have a lot of sugar?
What allergens are in carrot cake?
How much fat is in carrot cake?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical record F02010004 with confidence 0.95. Secondary nutrients (saturated fat, sugar, cholesterol, micronutrients) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central FDC 174929 (carrot cake with cream cheese frosting), an approved secondary source. GI value of 39 sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed publication. All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: vitamin_d_mcg set to -1: vitamin D data for carrot cake with cream cheese frosting is not consistently reported in either the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central FDC 174929 as of April 2026; no approved source provides a reliable value for this preparation. selenium_mcg set to -1: selenium data for carrot cake with cream cheese frosting is not reported in HPB Singapore Foodo (F02010004) and is absent from USDA FoodData Central FDC 174929 as of April 2026; no approved source provides a reliable value. Sugar value of 22 g/100 g is estimated from USDA FDC 174929 as the HPB source does not separately report sugar; this is consistent with typical carrot cake with cream cheese frosting formulations. Saturated fat of 4.5 g/100 g is similarly estimated from USDA FDC 174929. Glycemic load per serving calculated using a 90 g serving size: (39 × 27 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 10.5. The dietary_tags field in the input included 'plant_based'; this has been corrected to 'vegetarian' as carrot cake contains eggs and dairy. No Singaporean-dish multilingual naming requirement applies as this is a Western dessert; local_names fields are null by design.

Iteration 3 — Fixed g5_ontology_honesty: corrected ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' (not an allowed value) to 'closest_parent', which is one of the three permitted values ['exact', 'closest_parent', 'none'] per the validator schema.
